Video.summary: A woman who vanished reveals a dark secret.
Video.title: A Daughter’s Dark Confession

Video.summary: If you think pretty people are immune to heartache, think again. The headlines are filled with stories of striking women who were devastated by infidelity, despite their outward beauty. Dr. Phil sits down with Joey, a husband and father, who says he can’t pass up a pretty face. His wife, Shannon, says he’s slept with close to 100 women since their wedding day. The personal trainer justifies his actions by saying Shannon is overweight and not hot enough for him. Will he get a wake-up call about his cheating ways? Or will his wife take him back yet again? See the shocking text messages to Joey’s mistress, and hear Dr. Phil’s strong message for the hapless husband when a disturbing secret is revealed. Can Dr. Phil encourage Joey to choose another path and look beyond the physical?
Video.title: Beauty Isn’t Enough

Video.summary: t’s been almost four months since 5-year-old Summer Wells vanished from her home in rural Tennessee on June 15, 2021. Her parents, Don and Candus, say they believe their daughter was abducted. But, the couple say online bullies have accused them of having something to do with Summer’s disappearance. Don and Candus agreed to sit down with body language experts Scott Rouse and Greg Hartley, interrogators who have worked with the FBI, law enforcement and the military and have been referred to as a human lie detector, in hopes of clearing their name. Find out what the men think and if Dr. Phil agrees with their findings.
Video.title: Summer Wells Vanishes (Part 1)

Video.summary: A Meddling Mother-in-Law or a Lying Cheating Daughter-in-Law? Dr Phil delves into the family drama.
Video.title: Toxic and Offensive Inlaws

Video.summary: Lavena says her 17-year-old son, Trevor, has an out-of-control rage problem and fears he could snap and kill her, her husband, her 5-year-old twins -- or become the next school gunman. And, Karen says she’s at her wits’ end with her 15-year-old daughter, Nicole, who she says sneaks out of the house, uses drugs, skips school and recently stole her car. What’s at the root of the teens’ behavior?
Video.title: Tyrannical Teens

Video.summary: Are we raising a generation of ungrateful children? Melissa’s sisters say her sons are the most spoiled boys in North America. They get everything they want – all they have to do is whine. Melissa says her kids just like toys, and she thinks her sisters may be jealous of her lifestyle. Next, Dori admits that her 13-year-old son, Parker, is spoiled. Parker says he won't take no for an answer, and even has a strategy for getting everything he wants. Find out what's behind Dori's need to spoil Parker. Then, Joan says her 14-year-old daughter, Jacquie, is a snob, and her need for trendy clothes is turning her into a materialistic monster. Plus, Lauren spends her entire paycheck shopping, but with no money in her checking account, she’s afraid she could be headed for trouble. Her mom, Diane, says she's not worried, it's just Lauren's way of relaxing.
Video.title: Spoiled and Entitled

Video.summary: A divorced couple secretly remarries, but did they make a mistake- again?
Video.title: Make It or Break It

Video.summary: Many women are itching to run to the altar, but Dr. Phil’s guests say they’re ready to run for the hills! Kelly is a self-proclaimed runaway bride. She’s been engaged four times but says she gets cold feet before she makes it down the aisle. Rick, her current fiancé, proposed four years ago and is still waiting for her to set a wedding date. Kelly believes that most marriages end in divorce, and she doesn’t want to become another statistic. Should Rick wait for her to make up her mind, or should he cut his losses and move on? Then, Chad and Candi have been engaged for over five years and have two daughters, but Chad says he’s not ready to tie the knot. He says weddings are too expensive, but Candi says he racked up $49,000 in loans for a car, a camper and a new deck for the house. Will Chad break down and give his fiancée the wedding of her dreams? Plus, Dr. Phil talks to a woman who says her fiancé left her six days before the wedding and stuck her with a $150,000 bill -- an allegation he totally denies.
Video.title: Runaway Bride, Runaway Groom

Video.summary: Is there something you’ve been hanging on to for just a little too long? Clothes you haven’t fit into since high school? Boxes of stuff you haven’t looked at in years? Dr. Phil’s guests say their loved ones need to call the junk yard and “Get rid of it!” Kim says her husband, Paul’s, Star Warshobby has to go. With over 6,000 pieces in his collection, Paul spends 30 hours a week playing with his action figures, building models and talking to friends about Star Wars. He even had a light saber sword fight at their wedding, so shouldn’t Kim have known what she was getting into? Next, Larry and Sheila married four months ago, but Sheila says she had no idea she was getting hitched to a hoarder. Larry says she’s got it all wrong -– she’s the pack rat! With some of their favorite collections rolled out on stage, can Dr. Phil help them negotiate what goes and what stays? Plus, meet Terri, who has four storage units and two garages full of sentimental memories -– like a rubber chicken and her father’s spine X-rays! She spends so much money storing her stuff, that she can’t even afford health insurance. What’s at the heart of her hoarding? Don’t miss this hilarious look at how collections can get out of control!
Video.title: Get Rid Of It!

Video.summary: Dr. Phil wants a violent teen to meet someone who could change her life. How will she react?
Video.title: Destoni’s Drama: Will She Accept Help? (Pt 2)

Image.alt: Destoni’s Drama: Will She Accept Help? (Pt 2)
Destoni’s Drama: Will She Accept Help? (Pt 2)
Destoni’s Drama: Will She Accept Help? (Pt 2)
Destoni’s Drama: Will She Accept Help? (Pt 2)
Destoni’s Drama: Will She Accept Help? (Pt 2)
Destoni’s Drama: Will She Accept Help? (Pt 2)
Destoni’s Drama: Will She Accept Help? (Pt 2)

Video.summary: When he last appeared on Dr. Phil, Kip’s brazen and outrageous tirade toward his 17-year-old stepdaughter, Tory, shocked audience members and viewers. But the married father of two refused to back down. When Tory broke down in tears, Kip finally agreed to find a calmer way to express his frustration. Just weeks later, Tory’s mother, Vicky, says the surly relationship has not only gotten worse, the hostility has escalated, and her patience is running thin. She says both Tory and Kip refuse to seek help, and now there is a new bombshell that is making their home life virtually unbearable. What does Kip say is his biggest issue with his stepdaughter? And, will this family finally agree to accept help and repair what’s left of their relationship? Then, Joe, 34, became an instant father to two teenage daughters. One of the girls has run away, and the other feels that he treats her like a slave. Can they resolve their disputes and come together as one? (FOLLOW UP FROM 9093)
Video.title: My Teen Can’t Stand Her Stepfather

Image.alt: My Teen Can’t Stand Her Stepfather
My Teen Can’t Stand Her Stepfather
My Teen Can’t Stand Her Stepfather
My Teen Can’t Stand Her Stepfather
My Teen Can’t Stand Her Stepfather
My Teen Can’t Stand Her Stepfather
My Teen Can’t Stand Her Stepfather

Video.summary: Secrets are revealed in a family with a shopaholic wife and rageaholic husband.
Video.title: Family Secrets, Family Lies

Video.summary: Dr. Phil's guests haven't even been married a year and are already wondering if it’s time to call it quits.
Video.title: The Honeymoon’s Over

Video.summary: Do you have a nosy loved one in your life snooping through your things, questioning your every move and driving you crazy? Dr. Phil has advice.
Video.title: Mind Your Business

Video.summary: Do you have someone in your life who's so annoying and frustrating that you just want him or her to go away? What do you do when you're co-parents with someone you can't get along with? And, what if your daughter secretly dated, and then married, a man you despise?
Video.title: Butt Out!

Video.summary: The conversation continues with a woman who says her sister lied about health diagnoses for attention.
Video.title: Saving Shemida: What’s The Truth?

Video.summary: Statistics show that 2,000 teens get pregnant every day. Moms and dads: Watch this show with your teenagers, because it can impact their lives and yours! Two families face off over who should raise baby Miley. Paige, 17, and her boyfriend, Jordan, 18, are determined to raise their child on their own, even though they live with Jordan’s dad, Chris. Nikki, Paige’s stepmom, thinks she and her husband should raise the child, because they can better provide for her, and she wants Paige to break up with Jordan. Why does she believe that Jordan is brainwashing Paige? The teen dad says Nikki is the one who’s controlling, and he’s stepped up to the plate. Will the families come to an agreement? See what Dr. Phil tells them they must do to ensure Miley grows up in a healthy environment. Plus, find out the 10 things teens want their parents to know about teen sex.
Video.title: Fight Over Teen's Baby

Video.summary: Shot in the face, disfigured for life, the former teen victim comes face to face with the girl who pulled the trigger. Can she forgive?
Video.title: The Girl Who Shot Me in the Face

Image.alt: The Girl Who Shot Me in the Face
The Girl Who Shot Me in the Face
The Girl Who Shot Me in the Face
The Girl Who Shot Me in the Face
The Girl Who Shot Me in the Face
The Girl Who Shot Me in the Face
The Girl Who Shot Me in the Face

Video.summary: Are deadbeat dads actually dead broke dads? As the American economy struggles, men comprise most of the recent layoffs. In child support cases, are men and women treated equally in the courts, or are the laws out of touch with today's crisis? Dr. Phil's guests, along with Mel Feit, director of the National Center for Men, and Lis Wiehl, author and legal analyst, debate the issue that has many men feeling like the laws are against them.
Video.title: Fathers And Finance

Video.summary: Danielle says that her husband of nearly 10 years, John, has been living a secret life -- reaching out to men on Craigslist for sex and looking at child pornography. John insists that the child porn was accidentally downloaded, and while he admits to exchanging emails with men online, he maintains that he never met up with anyone in person. How does he explain his actions? And, what will the results of a polygraph test reveal?
Video.title: My Husband’s Secret Life

Video.summary: Dr. Phil takes goes inside the unthinkable: making your own child sick to fulfill a disturbing need for attention. The idea might sound unimaginable, but for people with a psychological disorder called Munchausen by Proxy, it's irresistible. Note: Viewer discretion is advised.
Video.title: Munchausen Mom

Video.summary: Dr. Phil debates viewers who disagree with the advice he gave to previous guests. One woman feels he had no right to push his view of adoption on pregnant 15-year-old, Alexandra. Another woman accuses Dr. Phil of bullying the participants on the Weight Loss Challenge and even accuses him of disliking fat people! What does Dr. Phil say about her inflated accusations? Then a 16-year-old bi-racial girl disputes Dr. Phil’s advice to a mother facing a racial identity crisis. How will these viewers fare when they go head to head with Dr. Phil?
Video.title: Debate Dr. Phil

Video.summary: What’s a woman to do when her spouse is constantly standing over her shoulder and nitpicking everything she does? Michelle says her husband, Jim, has controlled her for the 15 years that they’ve been together, and she’s fed up with his faultfinding. She says he tells her what to wear, how much she should eat, and even uses a GPS to track her whereabouts! Jim admits that his behavior is out of control and fears that he could be pushing his wife out the door. Why is he so critical of Michelle, and can Dr. Phil get the judgmental hubby to change his ways? Then, Sally says her husband, John, is so controlling that she spends most of her days picking up dust bunnies from the floor and only speaking when he allows her to. John says cleanliness is next to godliness and believes control is a matter of perception. See what causes him to go toe to toe with Dr. Phil! Will the couple get the help they’re seeking? It’s an explosive Dr. Phil you don’t want to miss!
Video.title: Bossy Husbands

Video.summary: A self destructive teen or a bad role model for a Mom? Who is telling the truth?
Video.title: Teenage Wasteland

Video.summary: Samantha and her sister Sarah say their mother, Stacy, is very successful as a special education teacher and behaviorist, but hey claim their mother has allowed their 33 year old brother Ben to live rent free for the past 2 years, while he lays by the pool. Stacy’s husband, Doug, says he moved out and refuses to come home until Stacy kicks his stepson out of the house. Ben insists he is not taking advantage of his mother’s hospitality and kindness; he is just stuck in a rut.
Video.title: Mom Needs to Cut the Cord

Image.alt: Mom Needs to Cut the Cord
Mom Needs to Cut the Cord
Mom Needs to Cut the Cord
Mom Needs to Cut the Cord
Mom Needs to Cut the Cord
Mom Needs to Cut the Cord
Mom Needs to Cut the Cord

Video.summary: Daniel Pelosi was convicted of second-degree murder in the slaying of investment banker and multimillionaire Ted Ammon -- the husband of his lover, Generosa. He is currently serving 25 years to life but now claims he is ready to explain what really happened the night of the murder. Dr. Phil visits Pelosi behind bars.
Video.title: High Society Whodunit

Video.summary: Demi Moore did it. So did Madonna and Halle Berry. This trend of older women dating younger men is called the Cougar Craze, and it’s sweeping the country. Recently, at a New York speed-dating event, "Sugar Mamas and Boy Toys," good-looking 35-year-old men wooed women over 35 who made at least $500,000 a year. Jeremy organized the event and says he was just answering a demand from affluent ladies. Was he sincere in his matchmaking or just out to make a buck? Dr. Phil talks to two women who participated in the event -- Gail, 44 and Nancy, 50. Their experiences might surprise you. Then, 51-year-old Kat says she’s no cougar; she just likes to date younger guys because men her age are set in their ways. She says her 23-year-old ex-boyfriend, Ryon, was loving, passionate and had a much higher libido than older men. Her friend, Nancy, says Kat has a great head for business, but a bad one when it comes to the opposite sex. She says young men just see dollar signs when they see Kat. Is Kat dating down, or should Nancy just butt out? And, Bobbi had an 11-year relationship with a man who was 17 years her junior, but she says once she turned 50, he saw her as too old. She wants to be in a relationship again, but not with an older man. Her son, Nathan, says someone still wet behind the ears can’t appreciate all Bobbi has to offer. Does Nathan have the right to put his foot down with his mother? Do you think it's OK for older women to date younger men? Take our poll!
Video.title: The Cougar Craze

Video.summary: Em says her husband, Shawn, has cheated on her with at least 78 women -- so why does she keep taking him back? Shawn admits to at least 20 affairs but says it’s his wife’s fault that he strays.
Video.title: Why Men Cheat

Video.summary: When you get married, you look forward to a life filled with love, happiness and security. But what if you find out your spouse has been telling you lies and is someone completely different than the person you thought you knew?
Video.title: Lies And Betrayal

Video.summary: Parents Liz and Jim say their 16-year-old son, Nic, has a serious drug problem that has such a hold on him, they claim he even stole $75,000 in gold from his parents’ safe and spent it all on drugs! But, Liz says she is convinced that her husband is to blame. Liz accuses Jim of being a narcissist and misogynist, whose parenting philosophy is based on intimidation and abuse, and claims that is what drove their son to drugs. Jim denies Liz’s allegations, claiming she has gone to great lengths to paint him as a villain. He says she is the major problem, and calls her the world's lousiest parent and a doormat who helped enable Nic's deadly addictions.
Video.title: Teen Drug Problem (Part 1)

Video.summary: A mother admits to putting hot sauce in her 7-year-old adopted son's mouth.
Video.title: Mommy Confessions

Video.summary: Lisa says her four beautiful adult daughters, Brandi, Wendi, Laci and Tahni, are "monsters" who pick on her, and their constant drama is tearing apart their family. However, all four daughters say it's Lisa who needs help. They claim she's a "textbook narcissist" who constantly plays the victim and has always put men before her family. Who is in the wrong here? Will Dr. Phil be able to bring this family back together?
Video.title: Narcissistic Mother
